mechanism_narrative: Sustained real GDP growth, measured by GDPC1, signals expanding aggregate demand which boosts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 positive returns over the following quarter.
evidence_refs: fact: Historical correlation between GDP growth and equity returns from 1950-2012
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in > 50% of quarters with positive GDP growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.5%, it signals labor market weakness that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact: Unemployment spikes in 2008-2009 preceded market declines
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate robust consumer and investment demand, reinforcing each other to drive strong corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS exceeds its 6-month moving average and HOUST exceeds its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact: Co-movement of consumption and housing historically leads economic cycles
failure_condition: The combined signal (RSAFS+HOUST above MA) yields a negative average forward 20-day return, or its Sharpe ratio is below 0.5 on an annualized basis over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle with high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), a rising industrial production index (INDPRO) indicates overheating that prompts further tightening, which compresses valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact: Tightening cycles in 2004-2006 and 1994 led to market corrections
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% and INDPRO rising, forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of observations, or the negative average return is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals increasing output and capacity utilization, driving higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns regardless of other conditions.
evidence_refs: fact: Industrial production leads earnings revisions cycle
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly gains fail to predict positive forward 20-day returns, with hit rate below 50% over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the federal funds rate below 4.5% signals the end of a tightening cycle or start of easing, which reduces borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact: Historical cuts in early 2001 and 2007 led to short-term rallies
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS first crosses below 4.5%, the subsequent 5-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of cases, or the average return is not positive (sign test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE) provides a robust labor market signal that boosts consumer confidence and spending, driving sustained positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month increase > 100,000 and UNRATE month-over-month decrease > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact: The 1990s expansion featured concurrent employment growth and falling unemployment
failure_condition: When the joint condition is met, forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remaining below a 2% year-over-year threshold indicates stable monetary conditions that support moderate growth and low discount rates, favoring positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact: The 1990s low inflation coincided with strong equity returns
failure_condition: When CPI yoy is below 2%, forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is below 0 over any 5-year window.
